narrative.executive_view: This screen concerns customer-specific software work, a labor-intensive service where AI tools are already common among developers but where tool use alone does not establish durable savings or customer willingness to keep outsourcing. The record therefore separates observed workflow change from the currently unmeasured commercial outcomes. [S1][S2][S3]
narrative.target_archetype: The selected archetype is a US customer-relationship-owned custom-development studio with repeat application support: it writes, modifies, tests, and supports software for particular customers, rather than selling packaged software or operating client facilities. Census's definition supports that activity boundary, but no defensible public source splits the supplied $1–10M EBITDA band into this narrower recurring-support archetype. [S1]
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI is most relevant to coding, test creation, documentation, routine analysis, status work, and support triage; it is less complete for requirements discovery, legacy-context judgment, architecture tradeoffs, client escalation, and production accountability. Stack Overflow reports frequent tool use among professional developers, while DORA reports broad use and productivity gains alongside material distrust of generated code. [S2][S3]
narrative.value_capture: Gross workflow compression is not the same as retained operator value. The selected studio may keep savings where it sells a fixed scope, production accountability, or ongoing support; it may pass them through where a client buys hours, named FTE capacity, or a competitively rebid scope. No reopened public source measured the 24-month retained-savings share for this lower-middle-market archetype, so it is left unmeasured.
narrative.adoption_timing: The evidence supports widespread developer AI-tool use, not the required point at which half of this specific archetype materially uses AI across the tasks represented in the labor estimate. DORA also conditions outcomes on practices such as platforms, testing, version control, and feedback loops. Accordingly, no target-archetype t50 estimate is asserted. [S2][S3]
narrative.buyability: The deterministic band is large, but the record does not equate all of it with a relationship-owned recurring-support studio. No reopened evidence established the owner's succession posture for this archetype or a reproducible count of active strategic and financial buyers for it. The seller signal is consequently neutral/unknown, and the buyer count is left unmeasured.
narrative.valuation: No public, reopened transaction set supplied comparable entry and exit EBITDA-multiple ranges for US $1–10M EBITDA custom-programming studios after separating staff augmentation, fixed-scope development, and recurring support. A market multiple cannot be inferred from the existence of large public IT-services firms, so both multiple inputs remain unmeasured rather than using an invented spread.
narrative.operator_survival: At mature adoption, the studio is plausibly pressured rather than automatically eliminated: customer-specific software still requires modification, testing, support, and accountable production operation under the NAICS definition, yet AI can reduce the labor scarcity that historically supported outsourced delivery. DORA's finding that generated code still attracts limited or no trust for many respondents supports continued review and accountability, not a guarantee of independent-studio demand. [S1][S3]
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The key unresolved questions are the target-archetype share of the band, whether contracts decouple revenue from hours, the timing of material adoption rather than tool use, buyer crowding, and lower-middle-market valuations. Survey and technology-team evidence are broad proxies, not evidence for US owner-operated studios; the 2022 receipt base also predates the recent coding-assistant adoption wave. [S2][S3]

sources.title: U.S. Census Bureau NAICS description for 541511
sources.what_it_supports: The activity boundary: writing, modifying, testing, and supporting software for a particular customer, plus the distinction from systems design and facilities management.

sources.title: 2025 Stack Overflow Developer Survey — AI
sources.what_it_supports: Broad working-developer proxy for AI tool frequency and sentiment; the page reports 50.6% of professional developers use AI tools daily, not target-archetype material adoption.

sources.title: Google Cloud: 2025 DORA Report announcement
sources.what_it_supports: Technology-professional proxy for workplace AI use, reported productivity gains, trust limitations, and the dependence of delivery outcomes on team practices and control systems.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Broad developer and technology-team evidence supports meaningful AI assistance in software-delivery workflows, but does not directly measure the target archetype or each SOC. Fractions isolate task compression from pricing, adoption timing, buyer activity, and mature operator demand.
caveats: The supplied role mix is 4-digit 5415 rather than a 541511 studio-only payroll mix.
Role fractions estimate technically compressible work, not booked revenue, realized savings, or displacement.

role_judgments.rationale: Coding, tests, documentation, and routine refactoring can be assisted, while architecture, requirements, legacy context, and production accountability remain material.
Routine support triage and knowledge retrieval are amenable to AI assistance, but escalations and client-specific diagnosis persist.
Reporting, staffing administration, and delivery mechanics compress; accountability for portfolio, staffing, and escalation does not disappear.
Documentation and analysis drafts compress, while eliciting requirements and reconciling stakeholder constraints remain human-intensive.
Proposal preparation and research compress, but relationship selling and commercial negotiation remain largely accountable work.
Internal operations and reporting can compress, but executive client relationships and capital allocation remain.
Other computer occupations include technical production work that can be assisted, with the same context and accountability limits as development.
Status, scheduling, and coordination artifacts compress; client-facing delivery ownership remains.
Research, analysis, and draft recommendations compress, while organizational diagnosis and stakeholder alignment persist.
Test generation and routine QA work are AI-assistable, but acceptance judgment, integration defects, and release accountability persist.
The large unlisted wage tail is heterogeneous; this bounded midpoint avoids assuming it is entirely technical delivery or entirely back office.

target_archetype.name: Customer-relationship-owned custom-development studios with repeat application support
inclusion_criteria: US firms whose primary service is customer-specific software writing, modification, testing, or support.
Studios with a direct client relationship and repeat application-support or enhancement work alongside project delivery.
Operators intended to fall within the supplied $1–10M EBITDA band.
exclusion_criteria: Pure staff-augmentation or body-shop providers without a durable firm-level client relationship.
target_archetype.selection_basis: This is the investable form of the Census-defined activity because repeat support can preserve a firm-level accountable role beyond a one-off build. The NAICS boundary supports the activity definition, but does not quantify how many deterministic-band firms meet the relationship and repeat-support filters. [S1]

coverage.rationale: No reopened source quantified the narrower archetype as a share of the supplied $1–10M EBITDA deterministic band.
caveats: The full NAICS band must not be relabeled as this narrower recurring-support archetype.

retained_savings_share_24m.rationale: No reopened source measured gross AI-created operating savings retained after 24 months by this lower-middle-market archetype after client repricing, pass-through, vendor cost, and competition.
caveats: Tool-use and productivity surveys do not identify commercial capture under the archetype's contracts.

t50_years.rationale: No reopened source measured when half of the selected studios materially use AI in the represented tasks; broad tool-use rates do not meet that definition.
caveats: Daily or workplace tool use is not automatically effective adoption across delivery tasks.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: No target-archetype current effective-adoption percentage was found; the available surveys report tool use for broad developer or technology-professional populations.
caveats: The cited survey results are retained in the narrative as context, not converted into this effective-adoption input.

historical_analogs.value: DORA's 2025 evidence is a near-term workflow analogue: AI benefits depend on surrounding practices and can expose downstream weaknesses, rather than acting as a uniform labor substitution rate.
historical_analogs.rationale: Technology-team research is a relevant but non-identical proxy for studio delivery operations.
caveats: It is not evidence of lower-middle-market pricing capture or target ownership dynamics.

seller_supply_signal.rationale: No reopened owner-age, succession, or seller-supply evidence was specific to the selected studio archetype; UNKNOWN is the prescribed neutral signal rather than an adverse inference.
caveats: A founder-demographic prior would not establish seller availability in this target population.

active_consolidators.rationale: No reproducible, current count was found for strategic and financial buyers demonstrably active for the selected US lower-middle-market studio archetype.
caveats: Named large IT-services firms do not alone establish a count of active buyers for this size and revenue model.

buy_mult.rationale: No reopened source provided a defensible entry EBITDA-multiple range for this archetype after excluding non-comparable staff augmentation and different service models.
caveats: Public-company trading data and broad IT-services commentary are not lower-middle-market transaction evidence.

exit_mult.rationale: No reopened source provided a defensible exit EBITDA-multiple range for a scaled version of the selected archetype.
caveats: Assuming either expansion or compression from absent private transaction data would be unsupported.

pricing_structure.rationale: No reopened evidence quantified the selected archetype's mix of time-and-materials, staff-augmentation, fixed-scope, and recurring support contracts.
caveats: Pricing structure is discussed as a diligence question and is not treated as observed market fact.

operator_survival.rationale: The Census activity includes ongoing modification, testing, and support for a particular customer, while DORA reports that AI use and productivity coexist with substantial lack of trust in generated code and practice-dependent outcomes. That supports an accountable paid role as plausible but under material price and volume pressure.
caveats: Neither source measures mature outsourced-demand survival for independent US studios.
DURABLE and DISINTERMEDIATED are retained as plausible alternatives because client sourcing choices are not observed.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_timing_consistent: Role fractions represent task-level technical compressibility only. Broad survey adoption was not converted into t50 because the required effective-adoption threshold is not measured for the archetype.
cross_checks.capture_vs_survival_separated: The 24-month operator capture field is missing because pricing and repricing evidence is absent; the mature-demand survival field is a separate proxy judgment about accountable outsourced operation.
cross_checks.no_deal_evidence_in_VA: No buyer, seller, or multiple information was used in role fractions or adoption timing.
cross_checks.multiple_neutrality_acknowledged: Absent lower-middle-market multiple evidence is recorded as missing; the finalizer's neutral default is a deterministic missing-data convention, not a claimed flat-multiple forecast.

dataset_mismatch.description: The deterministic role mix is 4-digit 5415 while the target is a narrower 541511 custom-development studio archetype with repeat support; the supplied n_band is also based on pre-AI-vintage 2022 receipts.
sensitivity: Role-level fractions retain broad ranges and identify the 4-digit mix as a limitation.
Archetype coverage is missing rather than asserting that all deterministic-band firms fit the selected studio model.

market_boundary.selection_basis: The selected studio is narrower than the full NAICS code because a direct client relationship and repeat support are economically relevant; Census defines the code's programming, modification, testing, and support activity but does not supply the archetype share. [S1]

top_uncertain_inputs.range: Missing; finalizer applies its preregistered neutral sensitivity convention
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Commercial capture determines whether technical labor compression remains with the operator or reaches clients and competitors.

top_uncertain_inputs.range: Missing; finalizer applies its preregistered neutral sensitivity convention
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: The deterministic target pool cannot be assumed to consist of relationship-owned recurring-support studios.

top_uncertain_inputs.range: Missing; finalizer applies its preregistered neutral sensitivity convention
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Broad AI tool use does not establish timing of material task-level implementation in the target population.

top_uncertain_inputs.range: Missing; finalizer applies its preregistered neutral sensitivity convention
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: No lower-middle-market, revenue-model-comparable entry or exit multiple ranges were reopened.
